title: Mount succeed, but files not visible
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When starting a container with a directory mounted on the host, the files are not shown even though the mount succeeded and the folder path is correct

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. mkdir ${PWD}/volume

2. podman run -d -v ${PWD}/volume:/var/lib/registry/:z -p 5000:5000 --name k8terer-registry registry:2.7.1

3. podman pull nginx:latest

4. podman tag docker.io/library/nginx:latest localhost:5000/nginx:latest

5. podman push --tls-verify=false localhost:5000/nginx:latest

6. ls -hal ${PWD}/volume

**Describe the results you received:**

```
[root@crio ~]# ls -hal ${PWD}/volume
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root   6 Jun 19 09:51 .
dr-xr-x---. 4 root root 184 Jun 19 09:51 ..
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
[root@crio ~]$ ls -hal volume/
total 4,0K
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root   6 Jun 19 09:51 .
dr-xr-x---. 4 root root 184 Jun 19 09:51 ..
dr-xr-x---. 4 root root 184 Jun 19 09:51 docker

```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.12.1.2
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 0.12.1.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.12.1.2-2.git9551f6b.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: b909c9e1a3e8f14d5694a118fb9c0c0325a31d4f-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 66969600
  MemTotal: 510861312
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-59.dev.git2abd837.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 2146430976
  SwapTotal: 2147479552
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: crio
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.21.2.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 2h 12m 18.11s (Approximately 0.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This is running inside Virtualbox.

[root@crio ~]# cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Linux release 7.6.1810 (Core)

[root@crio ~]# sestatus 
SELinux status:                 enabled
SELinuxfs mount:                /sys/fs/selinux
SELinux root directory:         /etc/selinux
Loaded policy name:             targeted
Current mode:                   enforcing
Mode from config file:          enforcing
Policy MLS status:              enabled
Policy deny_unknown status:     allowed
Max kernel policy version:      31

SELinux didn't block anything:
```
[root@crio ~]# ausearch -m AVC,USER_AVC -ts recent
<no matches>
```

Inspection of the container:
```
        "Mounts": [
            {
                "destination": "/var/lib/registry/",
                "type": "bind",
                "source": "/root/volume",
                "options": [
                    "rbind",
                    "rw",
                    "rprivate"
                ]
            },
```

If I take a look inside the container itself I can see the data:
```
[root@crio ~]# podman exec -it k8terer-registry ls -hal /var/lib/registry
total 0
drwxr-xr-x    3 root     root          20 Jun 19 09:52 .
drwxr-xr-x    1 root     root          22 Jun 19 09:51 ..
drwxr-xr-x    3 root     root          22 Jun 19 09:52 docker
```

comments.body:
Apparently after trying to run it outside of Virtualbox/Centos on my Ubuntu 19.04 host, it works, and that's probably due to the waaay more newer version of Podman. *sigh* Centos and older software...

Podman version on my host machine where it works:
```
Version:            1.4.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
Since this is a problem resolved by a newer version of podman, closing this issue.

The good news is that the next CentOS minor release should have 1.4.0, and
I think we'll be getting 1.3.2 out in a point release before that.

I just realized that podman 1.3.2 is actually present in the Centos Extra repository and I don't do a yum clean all before installing podman, causing the older version to be installed in Virtualbox through Vagrant. So it's actually an already solved problem :)
